title: Items_page style filtering on sub_items
excerpt: The filtering that items_page offers in the 2023-10 api is very nice.  I find myself wishing that I could apply the same filtering on column values to sub_items.  In fact, in some cases I have found the complexity to be …

posts.author: drescheg (Guy Drescher)
posts.cookedHtml: The filtering that items_page offers in the 2023-10 api is very nice. I find myself wishing that I could apply the same filtering on column values to sub_items. In fact, in some cases I have found the complexity to be less if I use items_page on the sub_items board and then retrieve the parent_item values as needed. Which seems a bit backwards and having the ability to filter on both seems like it could be the most efficient.

posts.author: joseph.monday (Joseph Laux)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Guy,

To clarify, have you used our subitems documentation here? Or are you asking about being able to use items_page in a similar fashion to query subitems?

Best,
Joseph

posts.author: drescheg (Guy Drescher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Using items_page to query and filter item and subitems columns at the same time. Maybe something like this:

query {
  boards (ids:1234567890) {
    items_page (limit: 50, query_params: {rules:
       {column_id: "status", compare_value: [5], operator:any_of}
       {subitem_column_id: "timeline", compare_value: ["2023-06-30", "2023-07-01"], compare_attribute: "START_DATE", operator:between}
    }) {
      cursor
      items {
        id
        name
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
